body:
{
  "title": "Customer Call — Orbit Systems (2026-06-10)",
  "compiled_truth": "Initial scoping call with [Orbit Systems](companies/orbit-systems) to explore a potential enterprise pilot. [Seren Voss](people/seren-voss) led the conversation from our side, with [Paz Santos](people/paz-santos) handling the technical deep-dive. On the Orbit side, their VP of Platform Engineering (Daniil Markov) and a senior infrastructure lead (Cleo Tan) joined.\n\nOrbit's core problem is straightforward but painful: they run a distributed logistics network spanning ~40 regional hubs, each generating telemetry and shipment manifest data that needs to be reconciled centrally in near real-time. Right now they're stitching together three separate vendor solutions — one for compression, one for integrity verification, and one for transport — and the failure modes between those layers are killing them. Daniil described a recent incident where a checksum mismatch caused a 14-hour reconciliation delay across their eastern seaboard hubs. That kind of thing apparently happens monthly.\n\nSeren walked them through the single-pass primitives at a high level, emphasizing determinism and the elimination of inter-layer failure boundaries. Their team was clearly intrigued but cautious — Orbit has been burned by vendor promises before and Daniil pushed hard on whether we could guarantee bit-identical outputs across their heterogeneous hub hardware. Paz handled this well, explaining our approach to platform-invariant determinism without overpromising on edge cases we haven't tested yet (notably some of their hubs still run legacy ARM-based gateway nodes from 2019).\n\nKey outcomes from the call: Orbit wants to start with a limited pilot scoped to 5 hubs in their midwest cluster. They proposed a 90-day evaluation window, which Seren countered with 60 days plus an optional 30-day extension — mostly to keep our own roadmap pressure honest. Daniil seemed amenable. Cleo asked about API surface area and Paz promised to send over preliminary integration docs by end of week.\n\nOne open question: Orbit's compliance team will need to review our data handling posture before any production data flows through our stack. Daniil said he'd loop in their legal/compliance lead (name TBD) by next week. We should be ready for that conversation — Paz flagged that we need to finalize our SOC 2 narrative language before then.\n\nOverall vibe was positive. [Orbit Systems](companies/orbit-systems) feels like a strong fit for a flagship logistics case study if we can nail the pilot. Seren noted after the call that this could be a good proof point for the broader supply-chain vertical we've been eyeing.",
  "timeline": [
    "- **2026-06-03** | [Paz Santos](people/paz-santos) receives inbound inquiry from Orbit Systems via the website contact form; routes to [Seren Voss](people/seren-voss) for qualification.",
    "- **2026-06-10** | Initial scoping call with [Orbit Systems](companies/orbit-systems) — VP Platform Engineering and senior infra lead attend. Pilot scoped to 5 midwest hubs over 60+30 day window.",
    "- **2026-06-14** | Paz sends preliminary integration docs and API reference to Orbit's engineering team.",
    "- **2026-06-23** | Follow-up call with Orbit's compliance lead to discuss data handling and SOC 2 readiness.",
    "- **2026-07-08** | Pilot agreement signed; Orbit provisions sandbox access to midwest hub telemetry streams.",
    "- **2026-07-21** | First test data flowing through single-pass stack from 2 of 5 hubs. Early results promising — latency under internal targets.",
    "- **2026-08-15** | All 5 hubs online. Cleo Tan flags minor issue with legacy ARM gateway nodes; Paz works directly with their team on a patch.",
    "- **2026-09-05** | 60-day pilot review meeting. Orbit exercises the 30-day extension to run a stress test over peak shipping season.",
    "- **2026-10-02** | Pilot concludes. Daniil Markov presents internal recommendation to expand to full 40-hub deployment.",
    "- **2026-11-18** | [Orbit Systems](companies/orbit-systems) signs enterprise contract for full network rollout, becoming Anchor Codec's first logistics-sector production customer."
  ]
}
